title: Subquery in WHERE with IN
instruction:
A subquery can produce a list for the `IN` operator. The outer query checks each row against the inner result.

Run this.
successMessage: The inner query produces `[1, 3]`. The outer query returns users with those IDs. Bob and Dave have no orders.

title: Subquery with scalar comparison
instruction:
A subquery returning a single value (scalar) can be compared with `>`, `<`, `=`, etc.

Run this.
successMessage: The average price is ~39.99. Only Gizmo (99.99) and Gadget (49.99) exceed it. The subquery is evaluated once and its result reused for each row.

title: EXISTS
instruction:
`EXISTS` returns true if the subquery produces any rows. It stops as soon as it finds the first match — efficient for existence checks.

Run this.
successMessage: Legal has no employees so it's excluded. `SELECT 1` is conventional — we only care about existence, not the actual value returned.
